text: WarMonitor He’s probably bluffing again. If this were real, we'd normally keep a lid on it. ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 Israeli Channel 12: New details have emerged about the rescue of the American F‑15E navigator shot down over Iran. The officer was unconscious with a concussion and did not transmit a distress signal initially. He made first contact at 12:00 on Friday, climbed to the highest point to avoid detection, walked 10‑12 kilometres and hid in a crevice, from where he sent precise coordinates on Friday night. Israel refrained from striking in the area and provided intelligence. During Friday and Saturday, Israel was asked to assist with air superiority and struck relevant targets. U.S. forces seized a farm 18 kilometres from his hideout, landing two aircraft and small helicopters. The helicopters extracted him from the crevice and returned him to the farm. During takeoff, two C‑130s became stuck in sand; three smaller planes were called to evacuate the navigator and special forces. Fighter jets then bombed the two stranded aircraft to prevent capture. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇸🇾 Security sources to Al-Jadeed: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam and the Interior Minister are handling communications with international and regional parties to neutralize al-Masna’a crossing and ensure state centers are not targeted, but there is no official response or guarantee so far. @wfwitness

The government will handle…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian MP Abdolhossein Rouhalamini stated U.S. President Trump wants to destroy and “swallow” Iran to take its oil, and that the aim is to partition the country. He argued that Iran’s ethnic groups would not agree to division and that such threats are bluffs. Rouhalamini acknowledged that Iran’s air defence was weak, but noted that Tehran’s skies are no longer as they were a month ago. He claimed Iran hit a U.S. AWACS costing tens of millions of dollars, adding that no air defence is 100% effective – not even US systems – and that American soldiers are hiding in hotels out of fear. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇦🇪 "An incident" targeted the port of Khor Fakkan in the Emirate of Sharjah on Sunday, local authorities announced. No injuries have been recorded so far. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 The Israeli Air Force said it struck Hezbollah's alleged command centers in Beirut's southern suburbs, as well as two gas stations owned by the Al-Amana fuel distribution company elsewhere in Lebanon. The military said Hezbollah used the headquarters to allegedly plan attacks on troops and Israeli civilians, and that the fuel stations generated millions of dollars for the group's activities. They added that since the start of the war, the IDF has struck more than 15 Al-Amana stations, which are under US sanctions. @wfwitness

A 25-year-old British flight attendant was arrested in Dubai after sharing a photo of drone strike damage near the airport in a private WhatsApp group. He only asked if it was safe to pass through the airport. Authorities accessed the phone and made the arrest. He faces up to 2 years in prison, large fines, and possible deportation. Source: MEE

Nuno Felix RT @michaeldweiss : "The C.I.A. campaign aimed to spread word in Iran that the airman had been found and was moving out of the country in a ground convoy. The hope was that the Iranians would shift their search efforts from the place where the airman was thought to be, to the roads out of the region. The C.I.A.’s operation did appear to cause confusion and uncertainty among the Iranian forces hunting for the airman." https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/05/world/middleeast/cia-us-airman-rescue-iran.html?unlocked_article_code=1.YlA.UiHu.AB7XU1LL1n7_&smid=nytcore-ios-share tweet

One of the Spirit-030 systems was found by pilots from the 414th Birds of Magyar Brigade and later destroyed by a drone strike. https://twitter.com/wartranslated/status/2040833548458811524/video/1 tweet

OSINTdefender Asked about the recent bombing of the B1 Bridge between Karaj and Tehran in Northern Iran, President Trump said that the strike occurred when the two countries were close to an agreement on direct negotiations, stating, “But then they said they will meet us in five days. So I said, ‘Why five days?’ I felt they were not being serious. So I attacked the bridge.” U.S. President Donald J. Trump claimed in a several-minute-long phone interview this morning with Axios that the United States is “in deep and intense negotiations” with Iran and that a deal can be reached before his deadline expires on Tuesday. Trump stated, “The negotiations are going well, but you never get to the finish line with the Iranians,” adding that, “There is a good chance, but if they don't make a deal, I am blowing up everything over there.” - OSINTdefender tweet

BREAKING: CENTCOM finally admits: The U.S. successfully rescued two crew members from a downed F-15E in Iran and continues military strikes. Both aircrew recovered safely in separate missions. Aircraft was shot down during combat on April 2.

⚡️ 🇮🇶 🇮🇷 Iranian State media outlet IRNA released images showcasing the aftermath of the U.S. / Israeli airstrike on the Shalamcheh international border terminal on Saturday damaged passenger halls. One person was killed and two others wounded. After a brief interruption, pilgrim and commercial traffic has resumed, with cargo terminal operations continuing without issue. @wfwitness
Israeli fire services say 3 people missing in Haifa following Iranian missile impact; rescue will take hours.
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 About an hour ago, Iran launched missiles which directly impacted in occupied Haifa

⚡️ 🇮🇷 Iranian MP Abdolhossein Rouhalamini announced that the Iranian parliament will pass a law for the “smart management” of the Strait of Hormuz, allowing Iran to decide on passage with discounts, fees or denial based on policy. The government will handle technical decisions, while the Supreme National Security Council and a strategic council will decide on political aspects. “Iran has not closed the strait, but has made its management smart,” he said, adding that passage could be granted in exchange for the release of seized ships or payment of tolls. @wfwitness
Israeli fire services say 3 people missing in Haifa following Iranian missile impact; rescue will take hours.

Israeli public strongly backs the war, but confidence in outcomes is slipping. ~78% support strikes on Iran. Support for regime collapse goal falling (63% → 54%). Fewer believe war will seriously damage Iran. Society split on how long it can endure war. Source: INSS
